buildcraft.guide.book.meta.name: BuildCraft Configuration Guide
tip.item.goggles: Wear to see coloured robot zones painted on Map Locations in your inventory and assigned to nearby robots
tip.block.autoworkbench_fluid: Automates 2x2 crafting recipes that need fluids, using internal tanks and MJ power
chat.gateCopier.noInformation: This gate has no configuration to copy
chat.debugger.quarry: Debug: highlighting the chunks this quarry keeps loaded
chat.debugger.laser: Debug: highlighting the laser's cone (green = reachable, red = blocked)
tip.block.engine_creative:
Right-click with a wrench to change power output level
Does not overheat
tip.block.engine_rf:
Converts Redstone Flux to Minecraft Joules
Does not overheat
tip.block.engine_rf.rf:
Converts Redstone Flux to Minecraft Joules
Does not overheat
tip.block.mj_dynamo:
Converts Minecraft Joules to Redstone Flux
Does not overheat
tip.block.mj_dynamo.rf:
Converts Minecraft Joules to Redstone Flux
Does not overheat
tip.block.engine_redstone:
Slowly generates small amounts of MJ for free
Does not overheat
tip.block.engine_stone:
Burns solid fuels to generate MJ
Overheats if it can't output power
tip.block.engine_iron:
Consumes liquid fuels to generate MJ
Overheats if there is no coolant
tip.block.marker_volume: Link together with a wrench or marker connector to create rectangular areas or volumes
tip.block.marker_path: Link together with a marker connector to create directional paths
tip.block.quarry: Mines a marked volume until reaching bedrock
tip.block.mining_well: Mines straight down until reaching bedrock or a fluid
tip.block.autoworkbench_item: Automates crafting from a blueprint and MJ power
tip.block.pump: Pumps fluids from below into its tank; place on an oil spring to drain it
tip.block.flood_gate: Releases fluid from its tank into the world when powered
tip.block.tank: Stores up to 16 buckets of a single fluid; stacks vertically
tip.block.chute: Drops items into the inventory below; accepts MJ for faster insertion
tip.block.distiller: Distills heated oil into gas and liquid fractions using MJ
tip.block.heat_exchange: Transfers heat between hot and cold fluid streams
tip.item.water_gel_spawn: Throw at a water source to begin gelling the area

gui.list.nbt:
Require Exact Match
§7(Enchantments, names and stored data must precisely match)
gui.list.match.idle: Hover the leftmost slot of an Accept Variations or Accept Equivalents line to see what it matches.
gui.list.match.no_handlers_hint: Falls back to exact-item match only.
gui.list.match.precise_inactive: Note: Require Exact Match has no effect here. It only constrains the exact-match (no Variations/Equivalents) path.
gui.buildcraft.pipe_diamond.title: Filters
gui.buildcraft.pipe_diamond_wood.title: Wooden Diamond Pipe
gui.pipes.emzuli.title: Extraction Presets

advancements.buildcraft.pipe_diversifica: Place a item, fluid, kinesis and structure pipe
advancements.buildcraft.extended_logic.d: Use a gate with extra slots
advancements.buildcraft.sealing_fluids.d: Smelt a cactus and craft its dye to produce pipe sealant
advancements.buildcraft.pipe_logic.title: Pipe logic
advancements.buildcraft.pipe_logic.descr: Place a gate on a pipe
advancements.buildcraft.all_plugged_up.d: Attach one of each unique pluggable to a pipe
advancements.buildcraft.pipe_fanatic.des: Place down one of each type of pipe
advancements.buildcraft.categorizing_wit: Setup a sorting setup using the power of colouring
advancements.buildcraft.colorful_electri: Place a pipe wire of every colour
tile.engineIron.name: Combustion Engine
tile.engineStone.name: Stirling Engine
gui.buildcraft.stirling_engine.help: Burns solid fuel to produce MJ power. Heat increases output. Overheating may cause damage.
gui.buildcraft.combustion_engine.help: Burns liquid fuel to produce MJ power. Requires coolant to prevent overheating. Higher temperature fuels produce more power.
buildcraft.help.list.label.title: List Label
buildcraft.help.list.label.desc: Names this list. The label appears in the item's tooltip which can be useful for identifying lists present in a diamond pipe. It has no effect on what the filter matches.
buildcraft.help.list.slots.title: Filter Line
buildcraft.help.list.slots.desc1: Each row is one independent filter. An item matches the list if it matches any line's slots.
buildcraft.help.list.slots.desc2: In Accept Variations or Accept Equivalents mode (the T / M buttons), only the leftmost slot is available and when occupied, uses that item as a tag source to add additional related items. Items to the right of this slot are random examples, but not limited to what is shown, that would match the filter.
buildcraft.help.list.button.precise.desc: Items must match the slot exactly, down to the same components (enchantments, custom name, damage, etc.) Mutually exclusive with Accept Variations and Accept Equivalents.
buildcraft.help.list.button.by_type.desc: Matches items in the same category as the leftmost slot. Example: iron ingot matches all ingots, an iron pickaxe matches all pickaxes, oak planks matches all planks.
Hover the leftmost slot with the Match Info ledger expanded to see exactly which tags drive the match.
Matches items made of the same material as the leftmost slot. Example: iron ingot matches iron block / iron nugget / raw iron.
For items whose tags are all single-segment (most wood, tools, and food), this behaves the same as Accept Variations. The cross-form distinction only kicks in for structured tags like c:ingots/iron.
buildcraft.help.tank.generic: Clicking this with an empty bucket will empty this tank into the bucket, or using a full bucket will fill this tank from the bucket.
buildcraft.help.tank.fuel: This tank can accept any kind of fuel.
buildcraft.help.tank.coolant: This tank can accept any kind of fluid coolant, or ice blocks.
buildcraft.help.tank.residue: This tank will output residue if the fuel hasn't been processed (for example oil).
buildcraft.help.stone_engine.flame.title: Fuel Left
buildcraft.help.stone_engine.flame: Shows how much of the current fuel item remains. The flame shrinks as fuel is consumed.
buildcraft.help.stone_engine.fuel.title: Fuel Input
buildcraft.help.stone_engine.fuel: Accepts any fuel that a furnace does.
buildcraft.help.filtered_buffer.filter: Restricts what item or block can enter the buffer slot below.
buildcraft.help.filtered_buffer.main: Items or blocks matched against the corresponding filter enter and are stored in their respective buffer slot.
buildcraft.help.tank.generic_block.desc: Holds up to 16 buckets of any single fluid. Pipes can fill or empty it from any side.
buildcraft.help.chute.slots.title: Buffer Slots
buildcraft.help.chute.slots.desc1: Items dropped onto the chute from above land in these slots and are inserted into the inventory directly below the chute.
buildcraft.help.chute.slots.desc2: Acts as a four-slot buffer when the target inventory is full or temporarily missing.
buildcraft.help.diamond_pipe.filter.desc: Each row corresponds to one face of the pipe (top, bottom, north, south, west, east, from top to bottom).
Items matching any slot in a row are routed out the matching face. Unmatched items take any unfiltered face, falling back to vanilla pipe behaviour.
buildcraft.help.diamond_wood_pipe.filter: Defines what the wooden pipe will or will not extract from the attached inventory.
Whether the listed items are required or excluded is controlled by the mode button.
buildcraft.help.diamond_wood_pipe.mode.d: Toggles between whitelist (extract only listed items) and blacklist (extract anything except listed items).
buildcraft.help.emzuli.filter.title: Filter Slots
buildcraft.help.emzuli.filter.desc: Items matching any of these slots are extracted from the attached inventory.
buildcraft.help.emzuli.paint.title: Paint Buttons
buildcraft.help.emzuli.paint.desc1: Each filter slot has an attached paint colour. Items extracted through that slot are tagged with that colour as they travel down the pipe.
buildcraft.help.emzuli.paint.desc2: Coloured items are routed by matching segments of diamond pipe and by other emzuli pipes downstream.
buildcraft.help.assembly_table.input.des: Insert the ingredients listed by any unlocked assembly recipe. The table consumes them once it has accumulated enough MJ.
buildcraft.help.assembly_table.recipes.d: Recipes whose ingredients are present in the slots above. Click an icon to queue that recipe; click again to pause it.
Power is divided evenly across all queued recipes that have enough ingredients to start.
buildcraft.help.assembly_table.power.des: Shows how much MJ has accumulated toward the next batch of the currently active recipe.
Insert the item to be modified — usually a gate or chipset that has room for an upgrade.
buildcraft.help.integration_table.output: The finished integrated item appears here once the table has consumed enough MJ.
Shows how much MJ has accumulated toward the next integration. The table only consumes power once a valid recipe is detected.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.map.title: Zone Map
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.map.desc1: Live 3D top-down view of the surrounding terrain. Hold a coloured paintbrush on the cursor, then left-click and drag to paint a rectangular zone and right-click and drag to erase it.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.map.desc2: Scroll to zoom, drag with an empty hand to pan, and use the arrow keys to nudge the view. The readout shows the X, Y and Z under the cursor.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.paintbrushe: Holds up to sixteen paintbrushes. The dye colour of the brush you pick up selects which of the sixteen zone layers you paint on the map.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.import.desc: Place a paintbrush and a Map Location holding a saved zone here to read that zone back into the planner.
The zone is loaded into the layer matching the paintbrush colour, replacing whatever was painted there before.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.import_prog: Fills as the saved zone is read from the Map Location into the planner.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.import_resu: The Map Location is returned here once its zone has been loaded into the planner.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.export.desc: Place a paintbrush and a blank Map Location here to write a painted zone onto the map.
The layer matching the paintbrush colour is saved onto the Map Location, offset to its real world position.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.export_prog: Fills as the selected zone layer is written onto the blank Map Location.
buildcraft.help.zone_planner.export_resu: The finished zone map appears here. Pull it out with a pipe or by hand to use it elsewhere.
buildcraft.help.gate.trigger.title: Triggers
buildcraft.help.gate.trigger.desc1: Conditions watched by the gate. Drag a trigger from the right-hand list into a slot; the action on the same row runs while the trigger is satisfied.
buildcraft.help.gate.trigger.desc2: Multiple triggers on connected rows are AND-ed together — the action runs only when every connected trigger is true.
buildcraft.help.gate.action.title: Actions
buildcraft.help.gate.action.desc1: What the gate does while its row is active. Drag an action from the right-hand list into a slot.
buildcraft.help.gate.action.desc2: Connected rows share their actions: if any trigger on a connected group fires, every action in that group runs.
buildcraft.help.gate.connection.title: Row Connection
buildcraft.help.gate.connection.desc: Click the button between two rows to join them into a group. Joined rows share triggers and actions (AND across triggers, OR across actions).
buildcraft.help.gate.params.title: Parameters
buildcraft.help.gate.params.desc: Some triggers and actions take parameters — an item to compare against, a redstone level, a colour. Drag onto these slots to configure the adjacent statement.
buildcraft.help.heat_exchange.hot_in.des: Insert a heated fluid here — heated oil from a previous exchanger, or naturally-searing fluids from the Nether. The fluid surrenders its heat as it flows toward the opposite end and emerges at the Cooled Fluid Output one heat tier cooler.
buildcraft.help.heat_exchange.cold_in.de: Insert a coolant fluid (water) or a cool oil/fuel awaiting a heat boost. It absorbs energy from the hot loop on the way through and emerges at the Heated Fluid Output one tier warmer.
buildcraft.help.heat_exchange.heated_out: The cold input emerges here one heat tier warmer. Pipe it into a Distiller, or chain through another exchanger to reach searing temperature.
buildcraft.help.heat_exchange.cooled_out: The hot input emerges here one heat tier cooler after giving up its heat. Recycle into another exchanger's cold input, or pipe it to storage.
buildcraft.help.heat_exchange.progress.d: The centre wipe visualises the exchanger spinning up (PREPARING), running at full transfer (RUNNING), and bleeding off remaining heat after a fluid runs out (STOPPING).
Heat only flows while both inputs hold compatible fluids and the hot side is strictly hotter than the cold side.
buildcraft.help.distiller.input.title: Fluid Input
buildcraft.help.distiller.input.desc: Accepts the fluid to be distilled — typically heated oil from a Heat Exchanger. Horizontal pipe connections feed this tank. Cool (untreated) oil has no matching recipe and is not consumed.
buildcraft.help.distiller.gas_out.title: Gas Output
buildcraft.help.distiller.gas_out.desc: The lighter gaseous fraction collects here. In the world, pipes attached to the TOP face of the Distiller drain this tank.
buildcraft.help.distiller.liquid_out.des: The denser liquid fraction collects here. In the world, pipes attached to the BOTTOM face of the Distiller drain this tank.
buildcraft.help.distiller.process.title: Distillation Process
buildcraft.help.distiller.process.desc1: A bright pipe schematic means the Distiller is actively consuming MJ and producing both outputs.
buildcraft.help.distiller.process.desc2: A dim schematic means a recipe matches but at least one output tank is full — drain it (bucket or pipe) and the Distiller resumes.
advancements.buildcraft.poweringUp.title: Powering Up!
advancements.buildcraft.poweringUp.descr: Activate a Stirling/Combustion engine with a redstone signal
advancements.buildcraft.lava_power.title: Lava Power
advancements.buildcraft.lava_power.descr: Power a stirling engine with a lava bucket
advancements.buildcraft.to_much_power.de: Make an engine overheat and whack it with a wrench
advancements.buildcraft.ice_cool.title: Ice cool
advancements.buildcraft.ice_cool.descrip: Cool a combustion engine with something other than water
advancements.buildcraft.sticky_dipping.d: Discover the sticky nature of oil
advancements.buildcraft.refine_and_redef: Produce a tank full of every single type of oil and fuel
tile.autoWorkbenchBlock.name: Auto Workbench
advancements.buildcraft.lazy_crafting.de: Make the autoworkbench work for you
advancements.buildcraft.retired_hopper.d: Put the chute back to work
advancements.buildcraft.flooding_the_wor: Flood the area with a floodgate
advancements.buildcraft.draining_the_wor: Use a pump to drain fluids from the world
advancements.buildcraft.oil_platform.des: Start pumping up an oil spout
advancements.buildcraft.heating_and_dist: Create a complete oil refining setup with a Distiller and a Heat Exchanger
advancements.buildcraft.black_gold.title: Black gold
advancements.buildcraft.black_gold.descr: Completely pump out a large oil spout
item.blueprint.author: Author:
tile.buildcraftbuilders.library.name: Electronic Library
tile.architect.tooltip.allowCreative.1: Creative allows all blocks - Creative Mode only!
advancements.buildcraft.architect.title: Architect
advancements.buildcraft.architect.descri: Scan an area with an architect table and record it in a blueprint/template
gui.buildcraft.builder.contentsmode.incl: Fill container contents from captured items
gui.buildcraft.replacer.help: Place a blueprint in the top slot and two used single-block schematics below (left = block to find, right = replacement). Edit the name if desired, then click Replace. The single-block schematics are not consumed.
advancements.buildcraft.paving_the_way.d: Utilize the builder with a pathmarker to create a path
advancements.buildcraft.diggy.title: Diggy diggy hole
advancements.buildcraft.diggy.descriptio: Have a quarry of 64x64 or larger finish mining
advancements.buildcraft.start_of_somethi: Build an extra-large structure with the builder
advancements.buildcraft.building_for_the: Set a filler on a loop for long term automation
advancements.buildcraft.destroying_the_w: Have two or more 64 x 64 sized quarries running at once at full speed
buildcraft.help.charging_table.input.des: Place an energy-capable item here. The table transfers MJ from an adjacent laser into the item when RF auto-conversion is enabled.
Insert a blank redstone board to program it with the selected robot behavior.
buildcraft.help.programming_table.option: Click a board type to select it. The table crafts the programmed board once enough MJ has been stored.
Shows how much MJ has accumulated toward crafting the selected board.
buildcraft.help.programming_table.output: The finished programmed board appears here once enough MJ has been stored. Pull it out with a pipe or by hand.
buildcraft.help.packager.input.title: Paper / Package Input
buildcraft.help.packager.input.desc: Insert paper to fold the configured pattern into a package, or insert an existing package to unpack its contents back into the storage row.
buildcraft.help.packager.pattern.title: Package Pattern
buildcraft.help.packager.pattern.desc1: Click a cell to toggle it, or click while holding an item to set a ghost template. Lit cells mark which storage slots are packed.
buildcraft.help.packager.pattern.desc2: The pattern maps directly onto the nine storage slots below: the item in each enabled slot is folded into the package.
buildcraft.help.packager.storage.title: Storage Slots
buildcraft.help.packager.storage.desc: Holds the items to be packaged. Items in slots enabled by the pattern are consumed when a package is created, and unpacking returns items here.
buildcraft.help.packager.output.title: Package Output
buildcraft.help.packager.output.desc: Finished packages appear here. Pull them out with a pipe or by hand; the item carries the packed contents.
buildcraft.help.requester.requests.title: Request Slots
buildcraft.help.requester.requests.desc1: Set a ghost item in each slot to define what this requester wants and how many. These are templates only and are not consumed.
buildcraft.help.requester.requests.desc2: Delivery robots linked to a station read these requests and bring matching items from the network.
buildcraft.help.requester.items.title: Supplied Items
buildcraft.help.requester.items.desc: Items delivered against each request are stored in the matching slot here. A request counts as fulfilled once the stored amount meets the requested count.
buildcraft.help.stamping_table.input.des: Insert the item to be stamped. The table consumes MJ from an adjacent laser to process it.
buildcraft.help.stamping_table.output.de: Stamped results are placed here. Pull them out with a pipe or by hand to keep the table running.
advancements.buildcraft.precision_crafti: Start crafting with an assembly table
gate.name: %s %s Gate
buildcraft.help.dynamo.upgrades.title: Supported Upgrades
buildcraft.help.dynamo.upgrades:
Upgrades increase the speed at which energy is converted. Place iron or gold gears into these slots.
1 compatible gear per slot.
buildcraft.help.dynamo.battery.title: FE Energy
buildcraft.help.rf_engine.upgrades.title: Supported Upgrades
buildcraft.help.rf_engine.upgrades:
Upgrades increase the speed at which FE is consumed and MJ is produced. Place iron or gold gears into these slots.
1 compatible gear per slot.
buildcraft.help.rf_engine.upgrades.rf:
Upgrades increase the speed at which RF is consumed and MJ is produced. Place iron or gold gears into these slots.
1 compatible gear per slot.
buildcraft.help.rf_engine.battery.title: FE Energy
buildcraft.help.filler.excavate.title: Excavate
buildcraft.help.filler.excavate.desc: If the selected pattern should hollow out the shape and clear the interior before building.
buildcraft.help.filler.locked.title: Locked
buildcraft.help.filler.locked.desc: Shows a lock if the pattern is forced by a nearby pipe gate.
buildcraft.help.filler.mode.title: Control mode
buildcraft.help.filler.invert.title: Invert
buildcraft.help.filler.invert.desc: If the selected pattern should be inverted, building blocks outside the defined shape.
buildcraft.help.filler.pattern.title: Pattern Slot
buildcraft.help.filler.pattern.desc1: Drag a pattern from the right-hand list into this slot. The pattern decides which blocks inside the marker box are filled.
buildcraft.help.filler.pattern.desc2: A pattern is locked here if a nearby gate is forcing one (look for the padlock icon).
buildcraft.help.filler.params.title: Pattern Parameters
buildcraft.help.filler.params.desc: Some patterns take parameters — corner offsets, sizes, percentages. Drag values onto these slots to configure the pattern above.
buildcraft.help.replacer.preview.title: Blueprint Preview
buildcraft.help.replacer.preview.desc1: A 3D preview of the loaded blueprint.
buildcraft.help.replacer.preview.desc2: When both schematic slots are filled, the preview shows the hypothetical result of the swap. Nothing is committed until Replace is clicked.
buildcraft.help.replacer.snapshot.title: Blueprint Slot
buildcraft.help.replacer.snapshot.desc: Insert the blueprint to modify. The preview, summary, and Replace button all act on this blueprint.
buildcraft.help.replacer.name.title: Blueprint Name
buildcraft.help.replacer.name.desc: Rename the blueprint. Changes are kept client-side until Replace is clicked, at which point the new name is written along with the swap.
buildcraft.help.replacer.from.title: Find Schematic
buildcraft.help.replacer.from.desc: A single-block schematic identifying which block to find within the blueprint. Use the Architect Table or a Schematic to capture one.
buildcraft.help.replacer.to.title: Replacement Schematic
buildcraft.help.replacer.to.desc: A single-block schematic identifying the block to substitute in. Every match of the Find schematic in the blueprint becomes this block.
buildcraft.help.replacer.replace.title: Replace
buildcraft.help.replacer.replace.desc1: Apply the swap and write back to the blueprint. Available once all three slots are filled and the schematics parse.
buildcraft.help.replacer.replace.desc2: Also commits the current name in the text field. The original blueprint is overwritten in place.
buildcraft.help.builder.snapshot.title: Snapshot Slot
buildcraft.help.builder.snapshot.desc1: Insert a used blueprint or template captured by an Architect Table.
buildcraft.help.builder.snapshot.desc2: Building starts as soon as a snapshot is present, the area is in range, and the required resources are available.
buildcraft.help.builder.resources.title: Resource Slots
buildcraft.help.builder.resources.desc1: Place the blocks and items needed to build the snapshot. The Builder consumes from these slots as it places each block.
buildcraft.help.builder.resources.desc2: Pipes or hoppers can feed these slots from any side. Anything that does not match what the snapshot still needs is left alone.
buildcraft.help.builder.fluid_mode.title: Fluid Handling
buildcraft.help.builder.fluid_mode.desc1: Cycles how the Builder treats fluid blocks in the build area: Skip (leave them alone), Replace (place schematic blocks on top, waterlogging where supported), or Clear (sweep every fluid out before placement).
buildcraft.help.builder.fluid_mode.desc2: Under Clear mode, source fluids the Builder breaks are absorbed into the internal tanks below — one bucket per source. Overflow when the tanks are full is discarded.
buildcraft.help.builder.contents_mode.de: Toggles whether the Builder fills captured containers (chests, hoppers, barrels, shulkers, furnaces, dispensers, brewing stands, ...) with the items the Architect recorded inside them. Default: fill — and the recorded items appear in the required-resources list so the player sources them.

Switch to the barrier-overlay state to place every container empty: captured contents are stripped at place time and no longer count against the resources list. Cosmetic block-entity data (custom name, lock string, loot table seed, ...) is preserved either way.
buildcraft.help.builder.display.title: Required Materials
buildcraft.help.builder.display.desc1: Read-only preview of what the snapshot still needs to finish. Each cell shows one stack-worth of pending materials.
buildcraft.help.builder.display.desc2: Empty cells mean that material is fully satisfied. The list updates as the Builder consumes resources.
buildcraft.help.builder.tanks.title: Fluid Tanks
buildcraft.help.builder.tanks.desc1: Four internal tanks, eight buckets each. Provide fluids for fluid-block placements in the snapshot, and store source fluids the destruction lasers absorb under Clear fluid mode.
buildcraft.help.builder.tanks.desc2: Fill them via pipes, a bucket click, or by letting the Builder absorb source fluids it clears from the build area (Clear mode only).
buildcraft.help.architect.preview.title: Scan Preview
buildcraft.help.architect.preview.desc1: Rotating 3D preview of the area being captured. While idle, it tracks the current contents inside the marker box and refreshes every couple of seconds.
buildcraft.help.architect.preview.desc2: Once a snapshot has been written to the output slot, the preview locks to that snapshot so the captured result is visible at a glance.
buildcraft.help.architect.snapshot_in.de: Insert a blank template or blueprint. A template captures only block shapes, a blueprint captures full block states and tile data.
Scanning starts automatically once the input is present, the output slot is empty, and the marker box is valid.
buildcraft.help.architect.progress.title: Scan Progress
buildcraft.help.architect.progress.desc: Fills left to right as the Architect walks the marker box. On completion the captured snapshot is moved to the output slot.
buildcraft.help.architect.snapshot_out.d: The completed snapshot lands here. Take it out and feed it to a Builder, a Replacer, or another Architect.
While this slot is occupied the Architect stops scanning — empty it to rescan the area.
buildcraft.help.architect.name.title: Snapshot Name
buildcraft.help.architect.name.desc: Names the snapshot. The name is written to the tile immediately and travels with the resulting blueprint/template item.
buildcraft.help.library.list.title: Snapshot List
buildcraft.help.library.list.desc1: Every snapshot stored on this client. Click a row to select; the selected entry is what Upload sends to the server bank and what Delete removes from the local library.
buildcraft.help.library.list.desc2: Rows show the snapshot's name when the header is intact, otherwise its raw key.
buildcraft.help.library.download_in.desc: Insert a used blueprint or template whose data is not yet cached on this client.
The library looks the snapshot up in the server bank, streams the data back, and returns the same item through the slot at left.
buildcraft.help.library.download_arrow.d: Fills right to left while the server is transferring the snapshot's data to this client. The item is moved to the output slot at left on completion.
buildcraft.help.library.download_out.des: The downloaded snapshot lands here. Its data is now in this client's local library and can be read by a Builder, a Replacer, JEI, and so on.
buildcraft.help.library.upload_in.title: Upload Input
buildcraft.help.library.upload_in.desc1: Insert a used blueprint or template to start uploading the entry selected in the list above to the server bank.
buildcraft.help.library.upload_in.desc2: The input item is consumed as a payment token; the output slot at right returns a fresh used item keyed to the just-uploaded data.
buildcraft.help.library.upload_arrow.des: Fills left to right while this client streams the selected snapshot's data up to the server bank.
buildcraft.help.library.upload_out.title: Upload Output
buildcraft.help.library.upload_out.desc: A fresh used blueprint or template pointing at the just-uploaded server-bank entry appears here on completion.
buildcraft.help.library.delete.title: Delete
buildcraft.help.library.delete.desc: Removes the selected snapshot from this client's local library. The copy in the server bank is unaffected — re-download to restore.
gui.jei.transfer.buildcraft.missing: You don't have the required materials
